en.extract:
The Ambon white-eye (Zosterops kuehni) is a species of bird in the family Zosteropidae. It is endemic to Indonesia. They are found on the islands Ambon and Serman in Indonesia. Ambon white-eye birds are known for their distinctive coloring, including the white rings around the bird's eyes. The Ambon white-eye is near threatened due to habitat loss. 


Taxonomy
The Ambon white-eye appeared over two million years ago. The bird quickly migrated to many islands. The Ambon white-eye is also known as a monotypic species.


Description
The Ambon white-eye is a small bird measuring about 12 cm long. The top of the bird's head and underparts are olive green, with a black streak under the eye. The tail and wing feathers are black and brownish, merging with the olive green. The bird's chin, throat, and undertail is a bright yellow. The rest of the underbody and legs is a grayish- white with a black bill on the upper side and a greyish color below. The bird also has grey legs. Both of the Ambon white-eye genders look alike. 


Habitat
Its natural habitats are lowland forest, wetlands, and rural gardens.


Distribution of Migration
The Ambon white-eye birds are from the Ambon and Seram Islands in Indonesia. The Ambon white-eye does not migrate, as the weather conditions remain tropical and consistently warmer in their climate. 


Behavior
The Ambon white-eye is an omnivore bird. The bird feeds on insects, fruit, and nectar. The bird has a tiny tipped tongue. This allows the bird to collect nectar from flowers and feeders. Ambon white-eyes are normally seen in pairs or small groups of three or four. The species is known to be cautious or careful when eating. The bird's call is short with a single “teeu” note. 


Threats and Conservation
The Ambon white-eye is currently being considered as a near threat. The species is currently not globally endangered. The bird's habitat is slowly diminishing on Ambon island. This due to deforestation and loss of habitat. If destruction of the forest on the island continues, it could lead to population decline in the future.

The '''[[Ambon Island|Ambon]] white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops kuehni''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Indonesia]].{{cn|date=November 2025}} They are found on the islands Ambon and Serman in Indonesia.{{cn|date=November 2025}} Ambon white-eye birds are known for their distinctive coloring, including the white rings around the bird's eyes.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=BirdLife International (BirdLife International) |date=2023-03-01 |title=IUCN Red List of Threatened Species: Zosterops kuehni |url=https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22714155/223163203 |journal=IUCN Red List of Threatened Species |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250503092618/https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22714155/223163203 |archive-date=2025-05-03}}</ref> The Ambon white-eye is near [[Threatened fauna of Australia|threatened]] due to habitat loss.<ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |last=van Balen |first=Bas |last2=de Juana |first2=Eduardo |date=2020 |title=Ambon White-eye (Zosterops kuehni), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/ambwhe1/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.ambwhe1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref> 

== Taxonomy ==
The Ambon white-eye appeared over two million years ago. The bird quickly [[Bird migration|migrated]] to many islands.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=BirdLife International (BirdLife International) |date=2023-03-01 |title=IUCN Red List of Threatened Species: Zosterops kuehni |url=https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22714155/223163203 |journal=IUCN Red List of Threatened Species |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250503092618/https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22714155/223163203 |archive-date=2025-05-03}}</ref> The Ambon white-eye is also known as a [[Monotypic taxon|monotypic]] species.{{cn|date=November 2025}}

== Description ==
The Ambon white-eye is a small bird measuring about 12 cm long.<ref name=":1" /> The top of the bird's head and underparts are olive green, with a black streak under the eye.<ref name=":1" /> The tail and wing feathers are black and brownish, merging with the olive green.<ref name=":1" /> The bird's chin, throat, and undertail is a bright yellow.<ref name=":1" /> The rest of the underbody and legs is a grayish- white with a black bill on the upper side and a greyish color below.<ref name=":1" /> The bird also has grey legs.<ref name=":1" /> Both of the Ambon white-eye [[Gender|genders]] look alike.<ref name=":1" /> 

== Habitat ==
Its natural [[Habitat|habitats]] are lowland forest, wetlands, and rural gardens.<ref name=":1" />

== Distribution of Migration ==
The Ambon white-eye birds are from the Ambon and Seram Islands in Indonesia.{{cn|date=November 2025}} The Ambon white-eye does not [[Bird migration|migrate]], as the weather conditions remain [[Tropical rainforest|tropical]] and consistently warmer in their climate.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=BirdLife International (BirdLife International) |date=2023-03-01 |title=IUCN Red List of Threatened Species: Zosterops kuehni |url=https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22714155/223163203 |journal=IUCN Red List of Threatened Species |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250503092618/https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22714155/223163203 |archive-date=2025-05-03}}</ref> 

== Behavior ==
The Ambon white-eye is an omnivore bird.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web |date=2025-05-06 |title=This Non-Native Songbird Is Taking Over California—Will the Rest of the Country Be Next? {{!}} Audubon |url=https://www.audubon.org/magazine/non-native-songbird-taking-over-california-will-rest-country-be-next |access-date=2025-10-14 |website=www.audubon.org |language=en}}</ref> The bird feeds on insects, fruit, and nectar.<ref name=":2" /> The bird has a tiny tipped [[tongue]].<ref name=":2" /> This allows the bird to collect [[nectar]] from flowers and feeders.<ref name=":2" /> Ambon white-eyes are normally seen in pairs or small groups of three or four.<ref name=":1" /> The species is known to be cautious or careful when eating.<ref name=":1" /> The bird's call is short with a single “teeu” note.<ref name=":1" /> 

== Threats and Conservation ==
The Ambon white-eye is currently being considered as a near threat.<ref name=":1" /> The species is currently not globally [[Endangered species|endangered]].<ref name=":1" /> The bird's habitat is slowly diminishing on Ambon island.<ref name=":1" /> This due to deforestation and loss of habitat.<ref name=":1" /> If destruction of the forest on the island continues, it could lead to population decline in the future.<ref name=":1" />
